articles.title: 50 Mayon evacuees in Albay down with chickenpox
articles.description: CAMALIG, ALBAY, Philippines — At least 50 evacuees staying in evacuation centers here for more than four months now have contracted chickenpox or bulutong-tubig, local disaster officials said on Wednesday. Dr. Rommel Negrete, chief of the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (MDRRMO), said 19 families were affected by the contagious disease, which he said was aggravated by the prevailing hot weather. Negrete said infected residents were immediately transferred to isolation areas to prevent the spread of the disease inside evacuation centers housing families displaced by the unrest of Mayon Volcano. “Everyone is safe and being treated.
